approach: additive-only: a new layout/ capability node alongside mux/ and axi/; no existing frozen mux scenario is narrowed, so no Clearance floor fires

seam additions (ratio, env on SessionOpenOptions) are additive optional fields — no existing caller changes shape; Gap A already landed on main and this branch was rebased onto it rather than re-implementing it

scope carved at intake against the design doc: core only, layout export deferred (needs a new portable geometry-report seam verb), --if-populated and --dry-run cut (the design flagged both as open; cutting --if-populated drops the cwd heuristic the design itself calls its weakest joint)
self-assert spec gate within leash; hold impl gate for a cold judge pass before advancing status

why: cold sdd-spec-judge (independent subagent, fresh context) judged the new layout/ node — README.md use cases + layout.feature (46 scenarios) — backward against {oracle,builder,architect}: all PASS, ALIGNED true, zero failing scenarios, no open markers. It re-derived the desugar and ratio arithmetic itself and confirmed the even-horizontal 1/n comb and the tmux -l 1-ratio inversion. A build-to-learn spike had independently validated the same two numbers before the suite was written. Mechanical check-suite + check-spec-state green. Scope carved at intake: core only, export/--if-populated/--dry-run out. Project rolls implemented -> approved because the new node is spec-approved but not yet built.

cold sdd-impl-judge (fresh context, second round after a change verdict) re-derived every frozen scenario's oracle independently: all 46 pass with assertions that genuinely match their claims, zero failing, no absorption, no structural blocker. It hand-re-derived the ratio inversion and the desugar comb arithmetic rather than trusting the tests, confirmed herdr's worktree verbs emit no --env, and empirically ran an injection attempt through a real pty (bash and zsh) against the env-prefix shell-quoting — no injection or breakage. Three defects from the first round were fixed in code and mutation-tested; the frozen suite is byte-identical across both rounds. pnpm verify green, 370 tests.

note: layout export — capture a live region back into a template. Deferred at intake: both backends can report geometry (tmux window_layout, herdr pane layout) so it is reachable via a new portable report-region-geometry seam verb rather than a backend-specific one, but that verb is a second seam and export can never recover a pane's command anyway.

open --layout reports a null workspace even on a backend that has a real workspace tier, because the session adapter's open returns only a pane id and has no channel for a workspace binding. Only the worktree capability reports one. The frozen suite pins only the tmux case, so this is not a contract violation, but the manifest is less informative than the backend could support.

the pane abstraction's split-with-options contract (the pane to split, plus the ratio and env fields this CR added) is specified only through the layout suite; the mux node documents none of it, including the split-target field that shipped in an earlier change. Pre-existing and consistent with house style, but the mux-layer contract is undocumented at its own layer.

the walk's pane-count and depth ceiling is untested — an eight-pane pool is seven sequential splits and no backend's minimum pane size is known. The opt-in integration tier should push past four panes and find where it breaks. Carried over as the design's one remaining open question.

no compound test exercises the ratio degrade and the root-directory degrade in a single apply. The two guards are structurally independent (separate functions, separate flags), so this is a coverage nicety rather than a defect.
